title: Dubai tops Asian crypto hubs, Taiwan passes crypto laws: Asia Express
summary: Japan's SBI Crypto shuts down 12th largest Bitcoin mining pool in the world, Russia gears up to launch digital ruble despite EU sanctions, and more: Asia Express.

title: Bitcoin nears $63.5K into weekly close as trader warns of 'terrible' Monday
summary: Bitcoin reached its highest levels in nearly a fortnight over the weekend, but BTC price action faced an "absolutely terrible" Monday track record.

title: Tankers Cross Hormuz Via US-Protected Corridor
summary: Western navies continue to say that the threat risk in the Strait of Hormuz is substantial and that the center of the strait has been mined, as ships try to avoid the attentions of Iran's military. Bloomberg's Michael Heath reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Banks Slash Short-Term Debt Sales on Cheaper Forex Funding
summary: Indian lenders are pulling back short-term debt sales as the central bank’s recent push to attract foreign-currency deposits has opened up a cheaper and more durable source of funding.

title: Fake Mac Clipboard App Delivers New Password-Stealing Malware
summary: A new Mac infostealer dubbed PamStealer impersonates the open-source Maccy clipboard manager to steal passwords and more.

title: Bitcoin to $53K? Exchange Deposits Jump as Analysts Warn of Increased Volatility
summary: Bitcoin bounced back above $60,000 this week, but increased volatility might be on the way as crypto deposits to exchanges spike.

title: Nigel Farage failed to declare funding from crypto gambling figure convicted of fraud: Sunday Times
summary: Farage already faces a parliamentary standards investigation over an undisclosed £5 million gift from Tether stakeholder Christopher Harborne.

title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum’s next rebuild will rival the Merge, and take three to four years
summary: He said quantum safety has "shifted up a LOT in priority" and that Hegota, slated for later this year, will likely be Ethereum's last "pre-Lean" hard fork.

title: Returning to pre-Trumpian politics won’t save America
summary: The opening of the Obama presidential library set off a wave of nostalgia for an era that is not coming back
